name: Kiffles
recipeInstructions:
1. Cream the butter, sour cream, and sugar together until smooth.
2. Add the egg yolks to the mixture, then gradually add the flour.
3. Knead the dough with your hands until it is well combined, adding a little more flour if needed.
4. Divide the dough into 6-8 portions. Pat each portion into a flat rectangle.
5. Chill the dough for about an hour.
6. Roll out each section between sheets of waxed paper to form pie crust-like layers.
7. Cut each rolled piece into 8 triangles using a sharp knife.
8. Place half a teaspoon of prune butter on each triangle.
9. Roll up each triangle starting from the wide end.
10. Arrange the kiffles on a greased cookie sheet.
11. Bake at 375°F (190°C) until lightly browned. If desired, sprinkle with powdered sugar.
12. Note that these have very little sugar but are still delicious and can even compete with Kolaches.
